source_text.text:
[PDF_PAGE 128 | score=4] weavesang enables great co14 blizzard, or thaw conditions to be summoned. Hurricane-force winds can be sum- moned near coastal regions in the late winter or early spring. The summoned weather is not under the control of the caster. It might last but a single turn, in the case of a tornado, or for hours or even days in other cases. The area of effect likewise varies from about one square mile to 100 square miles. Note that several casters can act in concert to greatly affect weather, controlling winds, and work- ing jointly to summon very extreme weather conditions. Within four turns after the spell is cast, the trend of the weather to come is apparent-for example, clearing skies, gusts of wa
[PDF_PAGE 23 | score=2] Still Waves By means ofthis spell, the caster can change ld4 sticks.plusone stick per experience level, into snakes; thus, a9th-level priest canchange 1&13 sticksinto an equal number of snakes. These snakes attack as com- manded by the priest. There must, of come, be sticks or similar pieces of wood (such as torches, spears, ctc.) to turn into snakes. Such a stick cannot be larger than a staff. Sticks held hy creatures are allowed a saving throw equal to that of the possessor (for exam- ole. a soear held hv an orc must fail the orc’s savine a hostile spellcaster or elemental must make a suc- cessful saving throw vs. spell to disrupt the magi- cally calmed waves. Notes: Granted by Valkur, Cap
[PDF_PAGE 26 | score=2] Stone Trap - Dwarf Stone to Flesh (Alteration) Reversible Sphere: Creation Level: 6 Range: IO yds.ilevel Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 9 Duration: Instantaneous Area of Effect: 1 creature Saving Thmw: Special The stone toflesh spell huns any sofi of stone into flesh. If the stone object was formerly living, this spell restores life (and goods), although the sur- vival of the creature is subject to the usual System Shock survival roll. Any formerly living creature, regardless of size, can he thus returned to flesh. Ordinary stone can he tumed to flesh in a volume of 9 cubic feet per level of experience of the spell- caster. Such flesh is inert, lacking a vital life force, unless a life fo
[PDF_PAGE 66 | score=2] Thorn Spray \\ as an undead creature, speak with dead, and similar activities from affecting them. A helmed horror is not an undead creature. It cannot he tuned, affected or dispelled hy holy beings or things. The material components of this spell are a pinch of hone powder or a bone shard, a pinch of dust, and a fairly complete, nonmagical, non- aligned suit of metal armor, which serves a$ the body of the helmed horror. Notes: Granted hy the evil god Bane ofthe Fon- GOTTEN REALMS setting. . .. Helmed Horror AC 2: MV 12, fly 1 4+l/level; THACO 12; #AT 1; Dmg by weapon or Id4 (fist); SD innatefeatl~er.fall, immune to illusions, EnchantmenislCharm spells, and mind affecting magic, healed by mu
[PDF_PAGE 4 | score=1] most general version is given first, while variants cast hy other races or found in specialized cam- paign settings are given later. A name marked with an asterisk (*) is a cooperative magic spell in which a circle of priests combines their efforts (coopera- tive magic is discussed in this hook). School: Each school governs a type of magic, according to the type of energy its spells employ and the special practices and methods used in cast- ing them. The basic schools are: SCHOOLS OF MAGIC (PHILOSOPHY) Abjuration: Protective, warding, or banishing Alteration: Magic that changes physical prop- Conjurafion/Summoning: Magic that calls or Divination: Magic that uncovers what is lost or magics. e
[PDF_PAGE 5 | score=1] access to at least part of this sphere. Few priests have access to all four elements. Plant spells affect plants, ranging from simple agriculture (improving crop yields) to communi- cating with plantlike creatures. Powers of nature or agriculture often grant access to this sphere. Sun spells deal in the basic powers of the solar universe-the purity of light and its counterpart darkness. Weather spells enable the priest to manipulate forces of weather. Powers of nature and tempests often grant access to this sphere. iidic S the Sphere of Healing. Rangers have the spheres ofAnimal and Plant. the Sphere of Healing Rangers have the spheres ofhmal and Plant SPECIALTY SPHERES In general, specialty
[PDF_PAGE 11 | score=1] Spiral of Degeneration > c Spiral of Degeneration Spirit Animal Form Quest spell, see appendix. (Alteration) Sphere: Animal Level: 3 Range: Touch Components: V; S Casting Time: 1 hr. Duration: Id10 days Area of Effect: Person touched Saving Throw: None Planes, hut is caught up by the force ofthe spell and utterly shattered, or its energy is diverted to some foul use (such as to power a particularly monstrous spell of gigantic complexity). This spell is usable only on those who are ahout to die or who have died (helow 0 hit points, or below -10 hit points if that optional rule is in use) within one round per level of the caster. The sub- ject receives a saving throw vs. spell, which negates t
[PDF_PAGE 19 | score=1] Sprite Venom ~ Elf Sprite Venom - Elf (Alteration) Sphere: Combat Level 3 Range: Touch Components: V, S, M Casting Time: 5 Duration: 1 rd./level Area of Effect: 1 arrowilevel Saving Throw: None By means of this spell, the caster can create an ointment similar to that employed hy sprites to coat the tips of their arrows. For every level of the caster, the caster can create enough sprite venom to coat the tip of a single arrow, dart, needle, or quarrel. (Sling bullets and other missile weapons that inflict bludgeoning damage do not benefit from the appli- cation ofspiite venom.) Any creature struck by an arrow treated with sprite venom must make a saving throw vs. poison or fall into a deep sl
